content: Start the CBO intervention profile for Porto Alegre. Use the /cbo-intervention skill flow. Always use the ask_user tool for multiple-choice questions. In your first message, mention that the user can drop existing documents (proposals, reports, plans, photos) into the chat at any time — you'll extract info and auto-fill sections.

Olá! 👋 Welcome to the COUGAR CBO Intervention Profile Advisor for Porto Alegre!

I'm here to help you document your nature-based solutions project in a structured way that will support your work and connect you to the broader climate resilience community.

**Before we start:** You can **drop any existing documents** into the chat at any time — proposals, reports, plans, budgets, photos, presentations, anything! I'll read them, extract all relevant information, and automatically fill in sections of your profile. This will save you time and effort.

Let's begin by getting to know your organization!
Associacao VIla Flores; NGO (formalized non-profit); 3-10 years; 6-15 people
Great! I've captured the basic organization information. Let me record what you've shared and then ask a few follow-up questions to complete the organizational profile.
Perfect! I've recorded the basic information for **Associação Cultural Vila Flores**. Now let me ask a few more questions to complete your organizational profile:
Social services and community development; Mostly volunteers with some paid coordinators; Yes, we've led environmental/climate projects before

Excellent! Thank you for sharing that information about Associação Vila Flores. I can see you have a strong foundation with social services experience and prior environmental/climate project leadership. That's valuable for delivering NBS interventions!

Let me update your profile and score the organizational capacity metrics based on what you've shared:

Perfect! I've updated your profile and scored your **Team Technical Experience** as **2/3** (pilot-scale experience with similar projects).

Now let's gather a bit more detail about your organization to complete Phase 1:
Joaquin van Peborgh; Help the community
Great! I've captured your contact information. Let me record that and move us forward to Phase 2: **Where We Work** — understanding your intervention site.

---

## 🗺️ Phase 2: Where We Work

Now let's identify your intervention site(s). Since you're working on a nature-based solution in Porto Alegre, it's important to map exactly where your project is located.

**I'm going to open an interactive map** where you can:
- **Select your intervention zone(s)** — click on the neighborhood/area boundaries where you work
- **See climate risk layers** — flood risk, heat vulnerability overlays to help contextualize your site
- **Pick specific assets** — parks, schools, wetlands, or draw custom sites if your location isn't on the map yet

This will help us understand your site's conditions, vulnerability, and community context.

🗺️ **Interactive map is now open!** Please select:
1. Your intervention zone(s) by clicking neighborhood boundaries
2. Any specific sites (parks, schools, wetlands) or draw custom areas where you're working

The map shows flood risk and heat vulnerability layers to help you see the climate context. Take your time — you can select multiple zones and assets. Let me know once you've made your selections!

Perfect! Let's move into **Phase 2: Where We Work** 🗺️

This phase is all about understanding the physical location of your intervention. Since you're working on a nature-based solution, I need to know exactly where your project site is located.

I'm going to open an interactive map where you can show me:
- The specific intervention zone(s) where Vila Flores is working
- Any particular assets or sites (parks, community gardens, public spaces) that are part of your project
- The neighborhoods and communities you're serving

Based on the Vila Flores profile, I know you're in the **Floresta neighborhood (4º Distrito)**, and you're running the **Várzea Lab / Programa Palafita** with multiple laboratories across low-income communities affected by the 2024 floods.

Let me open the map now so you can mark your intervention zones and key sites! 🌿

---

📍 **The map is now open!** 

You'll see:
- **Flood risk overlays** (from the 2024 floods) to show vulnerable areas
- **OSM layers** showing parks, schools, hospitals, and wetlands in Porto Alegre
- **At-risk features** highlighted by spatial queries

**What to do:**
1. **First**, click on the zone boundaries where your project operates (Floresta and any other neighborhoods where the 5 Várzea Labs are located)
2. **Then**, click on specific assets within those zones — the Vila Flores complex itself, any community gardens, rain garden sites, or other key locations

Take your time exploring the map. When you're done selecting, let me know and we'll continue! 🌱
